currentUse: Active Roman Catholic parish church (Archdiocese of Los Angeles) with a weekly Lithuanian-language Sunday Mass, a Lithuanian pastor, a Saturday Lithuanian heritage school, and the annual Los Angeles Lithuanian Days festival on its grounds
summary: St. Casimir Lithuanian Catholic Church at 2718 St. George Street in the Los Feliz district of Los Angeles is standing and fully active as a parish of the Archdiocese of Los Angeles, with weekday Masses and three Sunday Masses including an 11:00 AM Mass in Lithuanian. The pastor is Fr. Tomas Karanauskas, a native of Kaunas, Lithuania, who also serves as spiritual director of the archdiocese's Lithuanian Ethnic Ministry. The church interior was renovated and rededicated in June 2022 with over $215,000 raised by the parish under the archdiocese's Called to Renew campaign, with new Lithuanian-themed sacred art dedicated by Archbishop Gintaras Grušas of Vilnius, a former parishioner. On May 31, 2026 roughly 400 parishioners, joined by Lithuania's Los Angeles consul general, celebrated an Atlaidai marking the parish's 85th founding anniversary and the 75th anniversary of the 1951 church building, with further Jubilee events — including installation of relics of Lithuanian blesseds — planned through November 2026. The parish continues to host Los Angeles Lithuanian Days, described as the largest Lithuanian festival west of Chicago, held October 5-6, 2024 and scheduled for October 11-12, 2025. The congregation has diversified to include Hispanic and Filipino parishioners, and diaspora observers note community concern about preserving the parish's Lithuanian character, but no closure, consolidation, or sale threat was found in any source.

developments.headline: Interior renovated and rededicated by Archbishop of Vilnius
developments.detail: The parish completed an extensive interior redesign — a newly dedicated altar, a mural of Blessed Teofilius Matulionis by artist Lalo Garcia, a map of Lithuania with six holy figures, a two-crown mural honoring St. Casimir and Our Lady of the Gate of Dawn, and a Hill of Crosses installation. The parish raised over $215,000 under the Archdiocese of Los Angeles's Called to Renew campaign; Archbishop Gintaras Grušas of Vilnius, a former parishioner, presided at the June 26, 2022 rededication.

sources.title: St. Casimir renovations celebrate Lithuania's Catholic spirit

developments.headline: Los Angeles Lithuanian Days held on parish grounds
developments.detail: The annual Los Angeles Lithuanian Days festival took place October 5-6, 2024 at St. Casimir Lithuanian Catholic Church; the parish has hosted the festival, described as the largest Lithuanian festival west of Chicago, since 1986, and the 2025 edition was scheduled for October 11-12.

sources.title: Los Andželo Lietuvių Dienos 2024 | Los Angeles Lithuanian Days 2024
Los Angeles, California: Lithuanians and Lithuanian heritage

developments.headline: Atlaidai celebration marks 85th parish anniversary and 75th of the church building
developments.detail: Approximately 400 parishioners celebrated an Atlaidai at St. Casimir marking 85 years since the parish's 1941 founding and 75 years since the 1951 dedication of the church building. Guests included Lithuania's Los Angeles-area consul general Sandra Brikaitė and honorary consul Daiva Navarrette. The celebration featured a Eucharistic procession, folk dancers, a bilingual English-Lithuanian Mass, and a Lithuanian beet-soup festival. Pastor Fr. Tomas Karanauskas announced further Jubilee Year events through November 2026, including installation of relics of two Lithuanian blesseds.

conflictsWithArchiveRecord: No contradictions. Findings confirm the archive record on every point: the 1951 building stands (Angelus News June 2026 celebrated its 75th anniversary), the parish remains inside the Archdiocese of Los Angeles (listed on lacatholics.org; renovation ran under the archdiocesan Called to Renew campaign, consistent with archdiocese title), and it operates as an active cultural center (Lithuanian Days festival, Saturday heritage school). Refinements to the archive's blanks: parish founded 1941 (85th anniversary in 2026); church dedicated 1951; the 2022 community-funded renovation ($215,000 raised by parishioners on archdiocese-titled property) echoes the archive's 'community-funded but archdiocese-titled' pattern for the 1951 construction.
gaps: The parish's own website (stcasimirchurchla.org) returned HTTP 403 to automated fetching, so Fr. Karanauskas's start date as pastor and the parish's own announcement text for the 2026 Jubilee/relics schedule could not be read directly; his identity as current pastor is verified via the June 2026 Angelus News article. No Archdiocese of Los Angeles restructuring or consolidation plan naming St. Casimir was found — searches for LA archdiocese parish closures 2025-2026 returned nothing implicating the parish — but absence of a published plan is not proof of zero exposure. The Global True Lithuania page's claim that the parish controls roughly 85 acres including a Lithuanian Hall acquired in 1948 could not be corroborated and the acreage figure looks implausible for Los Feliz, so it is excluded from the summary. Status of the former parish school building (whether a school still operates) was not verified, though the Saturday Lithuanian heritage school is confirmed active.
